summary: Implemented OpenCode-style lazy MCP init. (1) Increased MCP_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SECS from 15 to 30. (2) Refactored ExtensionManager::initialize() to connect all servers in parallel using futures::join_all — one slow server no longer blocks others. Extracted connect_server_standalone() as a free function so parallel futures don't fight over &mut self. (3) AgentStack::new() no longer calls init_mcp at startup; mcp field starts as None. Added mcp_init_done: Arc<AtomicBool> field. (4) Added ensure_mcp_initialized(&self) method that uses compare_exchange to guarantee exactly one background tokio::spawn — concurrent first-run() callers get a no-op on the second call. The background task connects all MCP servers in parallel, registers tools, and refreshes the permission middleware source map. (5) stack_run.rs run() calls self.ensure_mcp_initialized() at entry — first call spawns background init, subsequent calls are no-ops. (6) Added McpServerStatus enum (Connected/Disabled/Failed/Connecting) to MCPServerInfo. Status is set to Connected for active servers, Disabled for session-disabled servers, and Connecting when the lazy init has not yet completed (mcp_server_info_from_config path). (7) Web server run_server() comment updated to reflect that MCP no longer blocks startup at all — WebState::init calls AgentStack::new which returns immediately, and MCP connects lazily on the first /api/agent/submit call.

notes.parallel_init: join_all drives all server connects concurrently; each has independent 30s timeout via tokio::time::timeout
notes.once_semantics: AtomicBool compare_exchange(false, true, AcqRel, Acquire) guarantees exactly one spawn even under concurrent run() calls
notes.disabled_set: ensure_mcp_initialized snapshots disabled set as empty at spawn time; any post-spawn disable/enable is handled by reload_mcp() as before
notes.backward_compat: init_mcp wrapper removed (was unused); init_mcp_with_disabled kept and used by both lazy-init and reload_mcp
